description: Conditional scheduling ('if legal approves') where the condition is met mid-conversation. The event begins as hypothetical but becomes real when legal approval arrives and Alice confirms the meeting with 'let's make Monday real'.

events.title: Partner Announcement Kickoff
events.reasoning: The meeting starts as conditional ('if legal gives us the green light') but the condition is resolved mid-conversation when Bob reports that legal has signed off. Alice immediately converts the conditional to a confirmed event: 'let's make Monday real. Nine AM with Sarah's comms team.' Bob confirms he will send calendar invites and specifies the full attendee list.
events.assumptions: Monday = Feb 23; 1-hour default block; Sarah's full team not individually named so only Sarah listed by role

mock_llm_response.summary: One event extracted: partner announcement kickoff on Monday Feb 23 at 9:00 AM in Conference Room A. The condition (legal approval) was met during the conversation, converting a hypothetical into a confirmed scheduled event.

notes: Conditional resolved pattern. The model must track that the condition ('if legal gives us the green light') is explicitly resolved mid-conversation, converting the hypothetical into a real event. The pivot phrase 'let's make Monday real' is the key confirmation signal.
